title: MonoGame Math Arena (LhbMathArena)
summary: A headless deterministic mini-game (Math Arena) that stress-tests XNA/MonoGame 2D/3D math by running a scripted replay against a physics simulation. The task requires repairing intentionally hollowed Vector2, Vector3, Matrix, Color, Rectangle, and MathHelper public APIs in the MonoGame framework, fixing a Point.ToVector2 coordinate swap and a seed corruption bug in the game layer, completing the ScoringSystem and TransformSystem scoring pipelines, and implementing the SimWorld simulation loop and ReplayIO deserializer so the game produces deterministic, hash-verifiable output.
instructionPreview: You are given: - A MonoGame source tree at /workspace/MonoGame - A deterministic headless mini-game project at /workspace/game_project (Math Arena) - Replay and expected-output assets under /workspace/assets - Helper scripts under /workspace/scripts
instruction:
You are given:
- A MonoGame source tree at /workspace/MonoGame
- A deterministic headless mini-game project at /workspace/game_project (Math Arena)
- Replay and expected-output assets under /workspace/assets
- Helper scripts under /workspace/scripts

Math Arena runs fixed-tick physics simulation (entity spawning, impulse-driven motion,
velocity dampening, collision detection, path scoring, and arena scanning) and records
a deterministic event stream plus final aggregate state hash.

Your task is to complete a full bootstrap workflow:

Phase A - Engine and game repair, then verification
1) Complete intentionally hollowed Microsoft XNA Framework 2D math implementation
   (Vector2, Rectangle, and MathHelper public API surface used by the replay). The task
   contains large missing implementations. The existing code around the hollowed sections
   may also have potential correctness issues. You must retrieve code context across
   modules and restore correct behavior end-to-end.
2) Complete the game-side C# project under /workspace/game_project (replay runner, replay
   I/O, simulation world, scoring system). Together they are on the order of several
   hundred lines. The game is a partial skeleton with an intentional seed mismatch in the
   replay runner; final behavior must be deterministic and driven by Vector2, Rectangle,
   and MathHelper operations, not hard-coded end states.
3) Build the MonoGame DesktopGL framework and test host, then run a selected set of
   MonoGame framework unit tests and save a machine-readable report.

Phase B - Game replay verification
4) Run the headless mini-game replay twice using the same seed and replay input.
   The game must exercise vector math, rectangle intersection and union, MathHelper
   utilities, and Point-to-Vector2 conversion on the same call chain as the hollowed
   engine code.
5) Export final state JSON and its SHA256 hash for each run.
6) Confirm replay determinism (two same-seed runs produce identical state hash).

Phase C - Integrated acceptance
7) Produce all required output artifacts listed below.
8) Ensure outputs match expected integrity checks.

Required output files:
- /workspace/output/engine_checks.json
- /workspace/output/engine_test_stdout.log
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/math_events.jsonl
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/engine_trace.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/final_state.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/math_events.jsonl
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/engine_trace.json
- /workspace/output/game_final_state.json
- /workspace/output/game_final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/determinism_report.json

Rules:
- Automated grading (pytest) checks engine_checks.json, math event types, engine trace
  flags, replay determinism, and compares the run SHA256 to a reference value stored only under /tests
  (not in the agent workspace).
- Do not edit files under /tests.
- Do not bypass replay execution.
- Do not fabricate hashes; compute them from the actual JSON outputs you generate.
- The final hash must be computed from canonicalized JSON content.
- Do not replace vector- and rectangle-driven simulation with manual state mutation.
- Do not edit files under /workspace/game_project/tests (if present).
- Do not bypass engine-side missing implementations by stubbing outputs.
- Engine fixes must be made on the 2D math types used by the game.

moduleDag.description: A headless deterministic mini-game (Math Arena) that stress-tests XNA/MonoGame 2D/3D math by running a scripted replay against a physics simulation. The task requires repairing intentionally hollowed Vector2, Vector3, Matrix, Color, Rectangle, and MathHelper public APIs in the MonoGame framework, fixing a Point.ToVector2 coordinate swap and a seed corruption bug in the game layer, completing the ScoringSystem and TransformSystem scoring pipelines, and implementing the SimWorld simulation loop and ReplayIO deserializer so the game produces deterministic, hash-verifiable output.

nodes.label: MathHelper Utility Functions
nodes.description: Implements 17 hollowed MathHelper static utility functions: Barycentric, CatmullRom, Clamp (float and int), Distance, Hermite, Lerp, LerpPrecise, Max (float and int), Min (float and int), SmoothStep, ToDegrees, ToRadians, WrapAngle, and IsPowerOfTwo. These are foundational math operations used by Vector2/Vector3 interpolation methods, Matrix.Lerp, Color.Lerp, and the ScoringSystem path-scoring pipeline.

nodes.label: Vector3 Public API Implementation
nodes.description: Implements ~69 hollowed Vector3 static and instance methods: arithmetic (Add, Subtract, Multiply, Divide, Negate), geometric (Dot, Cross, Distance, DistanceSquared, Length, LengthSquared, Normalize, Reflect), interpolation (Lerp, LerpPrecise, SmoothStep, CatmullRom, Barycentric, Hermite), component utilities (Clamp, Min, Max, Ceiling, Floor, Round), transform (Transform, TransformNormal), and object model (Equals, GetHashCode, ToString, Deconstruct, ToNumerics).

nodes.label: Matrix Public API Implementation
nodes.description: Implements ~63 hollowed Matrix methods: arithmetic (Add, Subtract, Multiply, Divide, Negate), creation (CreateRotationX/Y/Z, CreateScale, CreateTranslation, CreateWorld, CreateFromAxisAngle, CreateFromQuaternion, CreateFromYawPitchRoll, CreateLookAt, CreateOrthographic, CreateOrthographicOffCenter, CreatePerspective, CreatePerspectiveFieldOfView, CreatePerspectiveOffCenter, CreateBillboard, CreateConstrainedBillboard), operations (Invert, Transpose, Determinant, Lerp, Decompose, Transform).

nodes.label: Color Public API Implementation
nodes.description: Implements ~21 hollowed Color methods and constructors: Color(float,float,float), Color(float,float,float,float), Color(int,int,int), Color(int,int,int,int), Color(Vector3), Color(Vector4), Lerp, LerpPrecise, Multiply, MultiplyAlpha, FromNonPremultiplied, ToVector3, ToVector4, GetHashCode, Equals, ToString, ToHSL, ToHSV, FromHSL, FromHSV.

nodes.label: Vector2 Public API Implementation
nodes.description: Implements all ~40 hollowed Vector2 static and instance methods: arithmetic (Add, Subtract, Multiply, Divide, Negate), geometric (Dot, Distance, DistanceSquared, Length, LengthSquared, Normalize, Reflect, Rotate, RotateAround), interpolation (Lerp, LerpPrecise, SmoothStep, CatmullRom, Barycentric, Hermite), component utilities (Clamp, Min, Max, Ceiling, Floor, Round), transform (Transform, TransformNormal), and object model (Equals, GetHashCode, ToString, ToPoint, Deconstruct, ToNumerics).

nodes.label: Point.ToVector2 Coordinate Bug Fix
nodes.description: Fixes a single-line coordinate swap in Point.ToVector2(): the skeleton returns new Vector2(Y, X) instead of new Vector2(X, Y), causing all point-to-vector conversions in the simulation to silently transpose coordinates.

nodes.label: Rectangle Public API Implementation
nodes.description: Implements all hollowed Rectangle methods: containment tests (Contains for int, float, Point, Vector2, and Rectangle), intersection (Intersects, Intersect), union (Union), mutation (Inflate, Offset), and object model (Equals, GetHashCode, ToString, Deconstruct). Also fixes an off-by-one bug in the Right property.

nodes.label: ReplayIO JSON Deserializer
nodes.description: Implements LoadReplay to read and deserialize the replay JSON file into a ReplayDocument (SpawnPlan + Impulses). The skeleton discards the path and returns an empty document.

nodes.label: ScoringSystem Path-Scoring Pipeline
nodes.description: Implements 8 hollowed scoring methods: ComputePathScore, ComputeInterpolatedScore, ComputeSplineScore, ComputeDistanceBonus, ComputeAngleBonus, ComputeVelocityFieldScore, ComputeCrossProductScore, and ComputeRadialScore. Uses MathHelper (Distance, SmoothStep, Clamp, Lerp, Hermite, CatmullRom, Barycentric, ToRadians, WrapAngle, ToDegrees) and Vector3 (Distance, Normalize, Dot, Cross).

nodes.label: TransformSystem Scoring Pipeline
nodes.description: Implements 9 hollowed scoring methods: ComputeRotationScore, ComputeScaleScore, ComputeTransformChainScore, ComputeDepthScore, ComputeEntityColor, ComputeColorBlendScore, ComputeProjectionScore, ComputeBillboardScore, ComputeOrthographicScore. Uses Matrix (CreateRotationZ, CreateScale, CreateTranslation, Multiply, Invert, Determinant, Lerp, CreatePerspectiveFieldOfView, CreateOrthographic, CreateBillboard, CreateLookAt, CreateOrthographicOffCenter), Vector3 (Transform, Dot, Distance, Normalize, Cross, Lerp, SmoothStep), and Color (Lerp, constructor, ToVector3).

nodes.label: SimWorld Simulation Loop
nodes.description: Implements SimWorld.Run() to execute the per-tick simulation: spawn entities, apply velocity impulses, integrate motion, dampen velocities, resolve collisions, compute path scores, compute transform scores, and scan arena. Also fixes a struct mutation bug and removes an unused Random field.

nodes.label: ReplayRunner Seed Bug Fix
nodes.description: Removes the `seed = seed + 1` statement that corrupted the RNG seed, removes an unused import, and adds UsedMathHelper trace flag output.

edges.label: Vector3 Clamp, Lerp, SmoothStep, Hermite, CatmullRom, Barycentric delegate to MathHelper
Color.Lerp uses MathHelper.Clamp and Lerp internally
ScoringSystem.ComputeVelocityFieldScore and ComputeCrossProductScore use Vector3 operations
TransformSystem uses Vector3.Transform, Dot, Distance, Normalize, Cross, Lerp, SmoothStep
TransformSystem uses Color.Lerp, Color constructor, ToVector3
SimWorld calls Vector2.Add, Distance, Reflect, Dot, Normalize
SimWorld.ScanArena calls loc.ToVector2(); X/Y swap breaks probe coordinates
ReplayRunner creates SimWorld, calls Run() and BuildFinalState()
